content: Addressing global health burden requires multi-level strategies: (1) Universal Health Coverage—financial protection, quality essential services access, essential medicines availability; (2) Primary healthcare strengthening—comprehensive continuous care, community health workers, integrated service delivery; (3) Disease-specific initiatives—immunization programs (Gavi), HIV/AIDS treatment (PEPFAR, Global Fund), malaria control (bed nets, ACTs), tuberculosis DOTS strategy, neglected tropical disease mass drug administration; (4) Risk factor reduction—tobacco control (FCTC), salt/sugar reduction, air quality improvement, road safety measures; (5) Health system investments—workforce training, infrastructure development, supply chain strengthening, health information systems, emergency preparedness; (6) Social determinants—poverty reduction, education (especially girls), water/sanitation access, gender equity; (7) Global health security—International Health Regulations implementation, surveillance capacity, laboratory networks, rapid response teams. Cost-effectiveness guides resource allocation (DALYs averted per dollar). Equity-focused approaches prioritize worst-off populations.
